Your world with it
Where will
you take it?
Picture the motorhome in your life. Three adventures — choose yours.
New Forest Weekend Escape

The motorway disappears in the rear view mirror. The automatic gearbox makes every journey effortless. Within forty minutes, heathland, ancient oaks and beech trees line either side of the road. Dappled light, wild ponies drifting across the track, the world slowing to a pace you had forgotten existed. You pull into your pitch as golden hour settles over the treeline. The awning opens with one pull. Two chairs. A glass of something cold. Nobody needs to be anywhere.

As evening falls the blown-air heating hums quietly. You cook a proper meal on the gas hob while the forest darkens outside. This is the version of your life you’d been meaning to start.

“I hadn’t planned a single thing. The motorhome just made it all so easy. By 7pm we were having dinner with the forest right there in front of us — something I’ll never forget.”
Pembrokeshire Coast Bank Holiday Weekend

The storm rolls in overnight. Inside the Imala 720, you are warm, cosy and utterly unbothered — blown-air heating, double glazing, a full kitchen. Outside is the most dramatic seascape you’ve woken up to in years. Winter motorhoming is not about roughing it. It’s about choosing comfort without choosing indoors.

“We had the whole headland to ourselves. The motorhome handled the cold without a murmur. Most dramatic weekend of our lives.”
Scotland in August Family Tour

The Old Man of Storr rises from the mist as you crest the hill. The children go genuinely quiet — which hasn’t happened since you left home. This is Scotland doing what Scotland does: making you feel small, and richer for it.

Four seat belts, four berths. The rear lounge becomes a den for the kids each evening; the front lounge yours. The fridge-freezer kept everything fresh for days. The blown-air heating turned a cool Highland night into perfect sleeping temperature within minutes.

Ten days. Seven locations. Zero hotel rooms. Every morning a different mountain, loch or coastline through the same window — because the window came with you.

“Best holiday we’ve ever had. We actually talked — properly. No screens, no distractions. Just the view and each other.”
France & Spain Summer Road Trip

You board the ferry as commuters. You disembark as travellers. The Imala 720 rolls south through Normandy, Bordeaux, the Pyrenees and out into the hot light of northern Spain. The air conditioning handles the heat without complaint. The exterior locker swallows two bikes and a paddleboard.

Three weeks of sun, market towns, long lunches and evening swims — all from a home that moves with you, costs a fraction of hotels, and holds everything you need. The mains hookup charges everything overnight. The awning turns a parking spot into a terrace. You stop counting kilometres and start counting memories.

“Three weeks, seven countries, zero regrets. We kept saying we’d head home — then finding one more place we had to see.”

The Auto-Trail Story
Built for Adventure.
Designed for Life.

Auto-Trail began in the early 1980s as a motorhome repair centre. Founders Bill Boasman and Barry Holmes started designing their own coachbuilt motorhomes — and never looked back. Over 35 years later, Auto-Trail is the UK’s premier dedicated motorhome manufacturer, part of the Trigano Group, building vehicles at a state-of-the-art facility in Grimsby.

The Imala Range — Launched 2014

Created for a new generation of motorhomers — couples and families who wanted a fully equipped, precision-engineered motorhome without the premium price. Innovative layouts and traditional Auto-Trail build quality made the Imala an instant award-winner.

Imala 720 — Two Zones, One Family

At 7.06m long, it separates into two distinct living zones: a generous lounge-diner up front and a second rear lounge, divided by the nearside kitchen and washroom. Both convert to double beds at night, sleeping four as standard.

7.06mLength
2 ZonesLiving
4–6Berths
35yrsHeritage

“The Imala gets the basics right and excels as a brilliant family layout — a compact, luxurious year-round tourer.”

— Caravan Club Motor Caravan Design Awards
Grade 3 Insulation & Cube Construction

Auto-Trail’s Cube construction bonds the bodyshell to the chassis — making it one of the quietest motorhomes on the road. Grade 3 insulation keeps the interior at +20°C even when outside temperatures drop to ‑15°C.

The Fiat Ducato — Britain’s Favourite Base

Auto-Trail has worked with Fiat for over 20 years. The Comfort-Matic automatic fitted to this vehicle delivers a car-like drive. Cruise control, cab air conditioning and steering wheel controls come as standard.
